The right sleeve shows the national flag.<ref>{{cite web|title=El Salvador Mitre 2011/13 Home Jersey / Camiseta|date=3 August 2011|url=http://footballfashion.org/wordpress/2011/08/03/el-salvador-mitre-201113-home-jersey-camiseta/|publisher=footballfasion.org|access-date=4 August 2011|archive-date=25 March 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120325125010/http://footballfashion.org/wordpress/2011/08/03/el-salvador-mitre-201113-home-jersey-camiseta/|url-status=live}}</ref>

El Salvador and [[Mitre Sports International|Mitre]] announced a new partnership in 2008 that saw them supply the [[Central America]]n national football team with home and away kits, training, and bench wear until August 2010. Mitre, and their Panamanian partner, The Harari Group, designed the kit that El Salvador used. The kit was showcased by the team on February 11, 2009, as they started their [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|FIFA World Cup qualifying]] campaign against [[Trinidad & Tobago national football team|Trinidad & Tobago]] in the [[CONCACAF]] (Central-American Football Union) Hexagonal Cup.<ref name="Mitre">{{cite web | title=Mitre (El Salvador) | url=http://www.mitre.com/el-salvador.html | publisher=[[Mitre Sports International|Mitre]] | access-date=2010-11-11 | date=2009-03-09 | archive-date=28 October 2010 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101028122513/http://www.mitre.com/el-salvador.html | url-status=live}}</ref>

On 22 October 2010, the [[Salvadoran Football Association|FESFUT]] extended the contract with Mitre by four years.<ref name="Mitre 2014">{{cite web | title= Mitre and El Salvador extend partnership | url= http://www.mitre.com/news/mitre-and-el-salvador-extend-partnership/ | publisher= [[Mitre Sports International|Mitre]] | access-date= 2010-11-22 | date= 2010-11-02 | url-status= dead | archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20101223165249/http://www.mitre.com/news/mitre-and-el-salvador-extend-partnership/ | archive-date= 2010-12-23}}</ref> The first home and away kit made by Mitre feature a [[watermark]] of the country's national shield on the center of the shirt and some horizontal stripes along the kit. The current kit featured white remains along the neck, at the bottom of the kit, and over the shoulders. When this kit was introduced in 2009 it also introduced a new [[logo]] that replaced the typical logo of an "E"  and an "S" surrounded by a circle.<ref name="Kit">{{cite web |url=http://www.soccershopusa.com/Display_Product_Detail.asp?ProductID=3978&Cat1ID=10&Cat2ID=55&Cat3ID= |title=El Salvador Home Kit |publisher=Soccer Shop USA |access-date=2010-11-14 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110716101116/http://www.soccershopusa.com/Display_Product_Detail.asp?ProductID=3978&Cat1ID=10&Cat2ID=55&Cat3ID= |archive-date=2011-07-16 |url-status=dead}}</ref> Umbro has become the new kit supplier of the El Salvador national football team. Replacing Mitre, the first Umbro El Salvador football kits were released 15 June 2017, and were debuted in the 2017 Gold Cup.

===Coaching history===
Since the creation of the national team in 1921, several coaches have been in charge of managing El Salvador. From 1930 to 1935, [[Mark Scott Thompson]] was appointed as El Salvador's first ever manager. {{As of|2012|1}}, the El Salvador national football team has presented itself with 60 managers in the national team. It is reported that all 3 titles ([[1943 CCCF Championship|1943]],<ref name="CCCF 1943">{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cccf43.html|title=CCCF Championship 1943 (San Salvador, El Salvador, Dec 5–19)|date=2002-08-06|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2010-11-11|archive-date=2 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221202072542/https://rsssf.org/tablesc/cccf43.html|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Football at the 1954 Central American and Caribbean Games|1954]]<ref name="CAG 1954">{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cag54.html|title=Central American and Caribbean Games 1954 (Mexico)|date=2007-04-02|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2010-11-11|archive-date=25 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221225051555/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cag54.html|url-status=live}}</ref> and [[Football at the 2002 Central American and Caribbean Games|2002]]<ref name="CAG 2002">{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cag02.html|title=Central American and Caribbean Games 2002 (El Salvador)|date=2005-12-21|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=2010-11-11|archive-date=25 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221225051555/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesc/cag02.html|url-status=live}}</ref>) have been won by Salvadoran born managers. [[Conrado Miranda]] has managed in 4 different occasions and [[Armando Contreras Palma]] in 3. [[Chilean people|Chilean]] [[Hernán Carrasco Vivanco|Hernán Vivanco]] was manager when El Salvador competed at their first [[1970 FIFA World Cup|World Cup]].<ref name="Managers">{{cite web|url=http://www.elbaloncuscatleco.com/directores.html|title=Directores|date=2008-01-24|access-date=2010-11-11|publisher=El Balon Cuscatleco|language=es|trans-title=Managers|archive-date=16 July 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190716065644/http://www.elbaloncuscatleco.com/directores.html|url-status=live}}</ref> [[Mauricio Alonso Rodríguez|Mauricio Rodríguez]] managed to qualify El Salvador to another [[1982 FIFA World Cup|World Cup]]. Rodríguez participated at the 1970 FIFA World Cup.

{{div col|colwidth=20em}}
*{{Flagicon|USA|1912}} [[Mark Scott Thompson]] (1930–1935)
*{{Flagicon|ESP|1931}} [[Pablo Ferré Elías]] (1935–1938)

*{{Flagicon|ARG}} [[Rodolfo Orlandini]] (1949–1951)

*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Mauricio Alonso Rodríguez|Mauricio Rodríguez]] (1973–1974)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Conrado Miranda]] (1975)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Marcelo Estrada]] (1975–1976)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Raúl Magaña]] (1976)
*{{Flagicon|BRA|1968}} [[Pinto Beltrao]] (1976)
*{{Flagicon|URU}} [[Juan Ricardo Faccio]] (1977)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Julio Contreras]] (1977)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Ricardo Tomasino]] (1978)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Raúl Magaña]] (1979)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Salvador Mariona]] (1979)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Mauricio Alonso Rodríguez|Mauricio Rodríguez]] (1979–1982)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Armando Contreras Palma|Armando Palma]] (1983)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Raúl Magaña]] (1984)
*{{Flagicon|ARG}} [[Juan Quarterone]] (1984–1985)

*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Raúl Magaña]] (1987)

*{{Flagicon|YUG}} [[Miroslav Vukašinović]] (1988–1989)
*{{Flagicon|SLV}} [[Conrado Miranda]] (1989)
*{{Flagicon|YUG}} [[Kiril Dojčinovski]] (1989)

<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to injury.<br />
<sup>COV</sup> Player withdrew due to testing positively for [[COVID-19]] or having to self-isolate because of it.<br />
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br />
<sup>RET</sup> Player had announced retirement from international football.<br />
<sup>SUS</sup> Player is serving a suspension.<br />
<sup>WD</sup> Player withdrew for personal reasons.<br />
<sup>EXC</sup> Excluded from squad.<br />
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended.<br />
{{nat fs end}}

==FIFA ranking history==
The following is a chart of the yearly averages of El Salvador's [[FIFA ranking]].
{{#chart:El Salvador FIFA ranking history.chart}}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[CONCACAF Championship]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[1963 CONCACAF Championship|1963]], [[1981 CONCACAF Championship|1981]]
** {{bronze3}} Third place (1): [[1977 CONCACAF Championship|1977]]

===Subregional===
* '''[[CCCF Championship]]'''{{sup|1}}
** {{gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[1943 CCCF Championship|1943]]
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[1941 CCCF Championship|1941]], [[1961 CCCF Championship|1961]]
** {{bronze3}} Third place (1): [[1946 CCCF Championship|1946]]
* '''[[Copa Centroamericana|Copa de Naciones UNCAF / Copa Centroamericana]]'''
** {{bronze3}} Third place (6): [[1995 UNCAF Nations Cup|1995]], [[UNCAF Nations Cup 1997|1997]], [[UNCAF Nations Cup 2001|2001]], [[UNCAF Nations Cup 2003|2003]], [[2013 Copa Centroamericana|2013]], [[2017 Copa Centroamericana|2017]]
* '''[[Football at the Central American and Caribbean Games|Central American and Caribbean Games]]'''
** {{gold1}} '''Gold medal (1)''': [[Football at the 1954 Central American and Caribbean Games|1954]]
** {{bronze3}} Bronze medal (1): [[Football at the 1935 Central American and Caribbean Games|1935]]

===Summary===
Only official honours are included, according to ''FIFA statutes'' (competitions organized/recognized by [[FIFA]] or an affiliated confederation).
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
!Competition!!{{gold1}}!!{{silver2}}!!{{bronze3}}!!Total
|-
|align=left|[[CONCACAF Championship]]
|0||2||1||3
|-
|align=left|[[CCCF Championship]]{{sup|1}}
|1||2||1||4
|-
!Total!!1!!4!!2!!7
|}

;Notes
# Official subregional competition organized by [[Confederación Centroamericana y del Caribe de Fútbol|CCCF]], direct predecessor confederation of CONCACAF and the former governing body of football in Central America and Caribbean (1938–1961).

==Notes==
{{reflist|group="note"|refs=<ref name="Aggregate score" group="note">Some notes in this article indicate the two scores that add to the aggregate score; for example, (2–0,1–1) results in an aggregate score of 3–1—2–0 being the first match played and 1–1 being the second match played.</ref>
<ref name="Withdrew" group="note">El Salvador turned down an invitation from [[Brazil national football team|Brazil]].</ref>

<!-- The following references appeared in the reflist but were not used in the prior text. Please return them to the reflist once they have been correctly cited in the main article.
<ref name="First game" group="note">The final score of the first match has also been considered to be 3–0. Different texts of sports magazines and books on Guatemalan, Salvadoran and Costa Rican football history do not agree with the result obtained.</ref>
<ref name="Minute goal scored" group="note">Some notes in this article indicate the time at which a goal was scored; for example, (30') indicates that a goal was scored at the 30th minute. This may also be combined by other goals scored by one player; for example, (30',63') indicates that 2 goals were scored at the 30th minute and 63rd minute by one player—which should be noted right before.</ref>
-->
}}
